title: Wave 2 bulk source/sink bank additions across 16 LanguagePatterns

diff_stats.deletions_note: All 9 deletions are old field-value lines that were REPLACED with extended supersets — every original entry preserved. Verified by line-by-line inspection (see audit_diff_lines below). PURELY ADDITIVE per validator mandate additive_bank_change_only.

additive_count_summary.loc_delta_actual_note: Higher than dispatch-contract estimate because each addition includes a per-entry VULN-SOURCE-PARITY-V1 M2 doc-comment block (~5-8 lines per addition) explaining the rationale. The pattern-entry count itself (~42 added matchers) is at the upper end of the contract estimate's '~22 distinct + ~2 source + 4 in-place' projection.

remaining_failures.reason: ATOMIC fixture rewrite + Deserialize bank deferred to M4
ATOMIC fixture rewrite + Deserialize bank deferred to M4
Backtick subshell AST-inexpressible per FAI-v1 \bgets\b precedent
vuln.rs:368-370 .rs CLI dispatch bypass — out of vuln-source-parity-v1 scope
Compound: .rs CLI dispatch bypass + RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest reqwest::blocking::get qualifier gap

Source side now detected (std::getenv qualifier added). Sink (boost::archive::text_iarchive) still empirically returns sinks=[]. M1 line 88 anticipated this contingency: 'If sink still doesn't fire post-M2, will re-classify in M5 carry-forward.' Sink-bank pattern is present in CPP_AST_SINKS:2552 (raw-fallback ('', 'boost::archive::text_iarchive')) but does not fire on the constructor-call shape `boost::archive::text_iarchive ia(std::stringstream(d) >> obj);` — likely a tree-sitter-cpp parsing variance distinct from the FQN substring path. Out of M2 additive scope.

M2 added the FQN raw-fallback ('', 'new java.io.ObjectInputStream(') as M1 routed. Sink IS now matched at the call_expression descendant (`new java.io.ObjectInputStream(...).readObject()`), but extract_first_identifier_arg_ast returns var=None because the constructor's first argument is itself an object_creation expression (`new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes())`) — not a plain identifier. The descendant match→var→sink emission pipeline drops the sink at the var-extraction step. M1's diagnosis (line 161) predicted this: 'flow-propagation through .getBytes() rather than bank-pattern absence'. Var-extraction logic extension is out of M2 additive scope (would require modifying extract_first_identifier_arg_ast which is on the don't-touch list per task validator mandate).

Same root cause as java_deserialization_positive — fixture uses `new java.io.ObjectInputStream(new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes)).readObject()`. M2 added the FQN raw-fallback to SCALA_AST_SINKS Deserialize. Sink-pattern matches at the constructor descendant; var-extraction returns None for the same nested-constructor reason.

vuln_migration_v1_red.carry_forward_aggregate_count_revised_no: Revised from M1's projected 5 (1 Ruby + 4 Rust) to 8 — the additional 3 are deserialization tests where M1 routed to M2 expecting bank-pattern to fix, but empirical M2 verification shows the bottleneck is downstream of the bank (var-extraction for nested-constructor args, plus a tree-sitter-cpp parsing variance for boost::archive). Per M5 stop_thresholds carry-forward cap of 5, this is OVER cap by 3. M5 must reconcile or the dispatch-contract carry-forward cap must be relaxed.

26_to_30_RED_transition_GREEN.rationale: 21 of 24 M2-routed RED tests transitioned GREEN; 3 (cpp_deser, java_deser, scala_deser) revealed downstream bottlenecks post-fix that are not bank-additive. Per dispatch-contract M2 stop_thresholds variance clause: 'variance depends on M1 Rust disposition'. M1 categorized those 3 as M2-routable on bank-coverage hypothesis; M2 verification falsified for those 3, escalating them to M5 carry-forward. The 21 transitions still validate the M2 bank-additive thesis for 87.5% (21/24) of routed tests.

string_literal_regression_guards_GREEN.evidence: Of the 12 vuln_migration_v1_red.rs failures post-M2, NONE are string_literal_fp tests (all are *_positive). The 83 string-literal regression-guards remain GREEN.

binary_smoke_string_literal_fp.note: Original task spec listed /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.* fixtures; only 3 of those exist (.py, .go, .ts — all returning 0 findings). Used canonical repo fixtures as the more comprehensive surface.

audit_diff_lines.lines_starting_with_minus_audit: Each of the 9 deletion lines is a SINGLE-line struct field whose array literal value was REPLACED by a multi-line array literal that PRESERVES the original entry verbatim and APPENDS new entries. No bank entries removed.
audit_diff_lines.verdict: PURELY ADDITIVE — additive_bank_change_only validator mandate satisfied

ruby_io_popen_pre_existing_failure_note.status: PRE-EXISTING RED at HEAD pre-M2 (verified by reverting M2 changes and re-running)
ruby_io_popen_pre_existing_failure_note.evidence: Failure mode is `[ast_only] expected at least one UserInput source; got sources=[]` — the test exercises bare `gets` UserInput source detection and IO.popen ShellExec sink. The M2 changes do NOT modify RUBY_AST_SOURCES or the bare `gets` call_names entry. Confirmed pre-existing by inspecting the workspace test diff before/after M2 changes — same failure occurs on M2-clean tree.
ruby_io_popen_pre_existing_failure_note.out_of_scope: Not introduced by M2; not fixed by M2; tracked separately.

carry_forward_carryforward_cap_note.recommendation: M5 milestone or M6 CHANGELOG should reconcile the cap delta. Three options: (a) raise M5 cap from 5 to 8 in dispatch-contract.json (out of M2 scope); (b) M5 documents the 3 deserialization tests as a separate carry-forward bucket distinct from the 5-test cap; (c) post-M5 follow-on milestone (var-extract-nested-constructor-v1) closes the 3 deser tests by extending extract_first_identifier_arg_ast to descend through .getBytes()-style argument chains. Recommendation: option (b) for M5, option (c) as future milestone — variant (a) is structurally inappropriate because the 3 new carry-forwards are NOT type-X (AST-inexpressible) but type-V (var-extract gap) which is a distinct disposition class. Final 162/166 passing (one short of M1's 161/166 projection due to ruby_io_popen pre-existing failure being counted).

verifications_run.cargo_test_workspace_release_no_fail_fas: PASS overall (1 pre-existing RED in rr_module_function_integ_test::ruby_io_popen unaffected by M2)
